questions.q: In Hindu mythology, Airavata is best known as the mount (vahana) of which deity?
questions.explanation: Airavata is the celestial elephant who serves as the vahana of Indra, king of the devas.

questions.q: What kind of animal is Airavata in Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: Airavata is described in the Puranas as a magnificent white elephant, the foremost of elephants.

questions.q: What is the most commonly described colour of the elephant Airavata?
questions.explanation: Airavata is traditionally depicted as a pure white elephant, mount of Indra.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, from which great cosmic event did Airavata arise?
options: The churning of the ocean of milk
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Airavata emerged among the treasures of the Samudra Manthana, the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: Airavata is the mount of Indra, who holds what office among the gods?
questions.explanation: Indra, whose mount is Airavata, is the king of the devas and ruler of the heaven Svarga.

questions.q: In which heavenly city, the capital of Indra, is Airavata associated as the king's mount?
questions.explanation: Indra presides over Amaravati, the capital of Svarga, mounted upon the white elephant Airavata.

questions.q: Airavata is described in the Puranas as the foremost or chief of which kind of creature?
questions.explanation: Airavata is hailed as the foremost of all elephants, the king among them.

questions.q: Indra, who rides Airavata, wields which famous weapon in the Vedas?
questions.explanation: Indra, the rider of Airavata, wields the thunderbolt Vajra, celebrated in the Rigveda.

questions.q: Airavata is a celestial animal belonging to which group of beings?
questions.explanation: As Indra's mount, Airavata is a celestial creature of the devas, the gods of the heavens.

questions.q: The white elephant Airavata is associated with which natural element through Indra's domain?
questions.explanation: As the mount of Indra, lord of rain and storm, Airavata is connected with clouds, water, and the rains.

questions.q: Airavata is named in the Puranas as the guardian elephant (dikgaja) of which cardinal direction?
questions.explanation: Airavata is described as the guardian-elephant of the eastern quarter, the direction over which Indra presides.

questions.q: Which distinctive physical feature is often attributed to Airavata in Puranic description?
questions.explanation: Airavata is frequently described as possessing multiple tusks, marking him as no ordinary elephant.

questions.q: According to tradition, Airavata is said to draw up water and hold it in the sky, connecting him to which phenomenon?
options: The forming of rain and clouds
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Airavata holding water in the sky, linking him to the gathering of clouds and rain in Indra's storms.

questions.q: During the churning of the ocean of milk, which goddess of fortune arose alongside treasures such as Airavata?
questions.explanation: The goddess Lakshmi arose from the Samudra Manthana among the treasures, the same churning that yielded Airavata.

questions.q: Airavata serves Indra, whose central Vedic myth is the slaying of which drought-bringing serpent?
questions.explanation: Indra, the rider of Airavata, famously slew the serpent Vritra to release the imprisoned waters.

questions.q: In the Puranas, Airavata is counted among the treasures that emerged from the churning together with which wish-fulfilling cow?
questions.explanation: The wish-granting cow Kamadhenu is among the treasures of the churning of the ocean, as is Airavata.

questions.q: Airavata is the mount of Indra, who rules over which celestial realm?
questions.explanation: Indra, whose vahana is Airavata, rules over Svarga, the heaven of the devas.

questions.q: In Indra's heavenly capital where Airavata is kept, which wish-granting tree is said to grow?
questions.explanation: The Parijata tree, which also arose from the churning of the ocean, grows in Indra's heaven where Airavata serves as his mount.

questions.q: Airavata is the mount of Indra, while which deity instead rides the eagle Garuda?
questions.explanation: Vishnu rides the eagle Garuda, whereas Indra's mount is the elephant Airavata.

questions.q: Airavata's pleasure garden home in Indra's heaven is most commonly named as which grove?
questions.explanation: Indra's heaven contains the celestial pleasure garden Nandana, the paradise where Airavata bears the king of the gods.

questions.q: Airavata most prominently appears in which body of Hindu scripture?
questions.explanation: Airavata's description as Indra's mount and a treasure of the churning is drawn chiefly from the Puranas.

questions.q: The guardian elephants of the directions, of whom Airavata is one, are known by which collective Sanskrit term?
questions.explanation: The directional guardian elephants, including Airavata of the east, are called the dikgajas.

questions.q: In the lineage given in some Puranas, Airavata is sometimes said to be born of which sage's progeny tracing to Kashyapa?
options: From the brood of Iravati or the daughters of Kashyapa
From the egg of Vinata's line
From the matted locks of Shiva
questions.explanation: Some Puranic genealogies trace Airavata to the offspring of Kashyapa's daughters, his name linked to Iravati.

questions.q: The name 'Airavata' is traditionally connected to which root sense in Sanskrit?
options: Sprung from the waters or ocean
questions.explanation: The name Airavata is associated with the waters, fitting his emergence from the ocean of milk.

questions.q: In the episode where Indra rides Airavata and is cursed for disrespecting a garland, which short-tempered sage is involved?
questions.explanation: In a Puranic account, the sage Durvasa's garland is trampled by Airavata, leading to a curse on Indra.

questions.q: Airavata serves the same king of the devas whose pride was humbled by Krishna in which Puranic episode?
options: The burning of the Khandava forest
questions.explanation: Indra, the rider of Airavata, had his pride humbled when Krishna lifted Mount Govardhan to shield the cowherds from Indra's storm.

questions.q: In Buddhist and broader Indic tradition, the elephant comparable to Airavata bearing the king of gods is often called by which related name?
questions.explanation: Airavata appears in wider Indic tradition under names such as Erawan or Airavana, the multi-tusked mount of the king of the gods.

questions.q: Which other celestial being, like Airavata, is named among the treasures churned from the ocean of milk?
questions.explanation: The divine white horse Uchchaihshravas, like the elephant Airavata, arose among the treasures of the Samudra Manthana.

questions.q: Airavata is the mount of Indra, whose thunderbolt Vajra was forged from the bones of which self-sacrificing sage?
questions.explanation: Indra's Vajra was forged from the bones of the sage Dadhichi; Indra wields it mounted upon Airavata.

questions.q: Airavata is the guardian elephant (dikgaja) of the east; in the classical list, which elephant is named as the dikgaja of the west?
questions.explanation: In the classical scheme of directional elephants, Airavata guards the east while Anjana is the dikgaja most often placed in the west.
